title: [FIRRTL] Non-deterministic test failures on Windows
body:
I've recently setup an internal CI flow which builds CIRCT on a pretty beefy VM -- Windows and Linux. At some point in the last week (or so), some FIRRTL tests started failing with this signature:

```
FAIL: CIRCT :: firtool/split-verilog.mlir (449 of 496)
******************** TEST 'CIRCT :: firtool/split-verilog.mlir' FAILED ********************
Script:
--
: 'RUN: at line 1';   c:\__w\1\b\build\bin\firtool.exe C:\__w\1\s\CIRCT\circt\test\firtool\split-verilog.mlir --format=mlir -verilog | c:\__w\1\b\build\bin\filecheck.exe C:\__w\1\s\CIRCT\circt\test\firtool\split-verilog.mlir --check-prefix=VERILOG
: 'RUN: at line 2';   rm -rf C:\__w\1\b\build\tools\circt\test\firtool\Output\split-verilog.mlir.tmp
: 'RUN: at line 3';   c:\__w\1\b\build\bin\firtool.exe C:\__w\1\s\CIRCT\circt\test\firtool\split-verilog.mlir --format=mlir -split-verilog -o=C:\__w\1\b\build\tools\circt\test\firtool\Output\split-verilog.mlir.tmp
: 'RUN: at line 4';   c:\__w\1\b\build\bin\filecheck.exe C:\__w\1\s\CIRCT\circt\test\firtool\split-verilog.mlir --check-prefix=VERILOG-FOO < C:\__w\1\b\build\tools\circt\test\firtool\Output\split-verilog.mlir.tmp/foo.sv
: 'RUN: at line 5';   c:\__w\1\b\build\bin\filecheck.exe C:\__w\1\s\CIRCT\circt\test\firtool\split-verilog.mlir --check-prefix=VERILOG-BAR < C:\__w\1\b\build\tools\circt\test\firtool\Output\split-verilog.mlir.tmp/bar.sv
: 'RUN: at line 6';   c:\__w\1\b\build\bin\filecheck.exe C:\__w\1\s\CIRCT\circt\test\firtool\split-verilog.mlir --check-prefix=VERILOG-USB < C:\__w\1\b\build\tools\circt\test\firtool\Output\split-verilog.mlir.tmp/usb.sv
: 'RUN: at line 7';   c:\__w\1\b\build\bin\filecheck.exe C:\__w\1\s\CIRCT\circt\test\firtool\split-verilog.mlir --check-prefix=VERILOG-INOUT-3 < C:\__w\1\b\build\tools\circt\test\firtool\Output\split-verilog.mlir.tmp/inout_3.sv
: 'RUN: at line 8';   c:\__w\1\b\build\bin\filecheck.exe C:\__w\1\s\CIRCT\circt\test\firtool\split-verilog.mlir --check-prefix=VERILOG-CUSTOM-1 < C:\__w\1\b\build\tools\circt\test\firtool\Output\split-verilog.mlir.tmp/custom1.sv
: 'RUN: at line 9';   c:\__w\1\b\build\bin\filecheck.exe C:\__w\1\s\CIRCT\circt\test\firtool\split-verilog.mlir --check-prefix=VERILOG-CUSTOM-2 < C:\__w\1\b\build\tools\circt\test\firtool\Output\split-verilog.mlir.tmp/custom2.sv
: 'RUN: at line 10';   c:\__w\1\b\build\bin\filecheck.exe C:\__w\1\s\CIRCT\circt\test\firtool\split-verilog.mlir --check-prefix=LIST < C:\__w\1\b\build\tools\circt\test\firtool\Output\split-verilog.mlir.tmp/filelist.f
--
Exit Code: 3221225477

Command Output (stdout):
--
$ ":" "RUN: at line 1"
$ "c:\__w\1\b\build\bin\firtool.exe" "C:\__w\1\s\CIRCT\circt\test\firtool\split-verilog.mlir" "--format=mlir" "-verilog"
note: command had no output on stdout or stderr
error: command failed with exit status: 0xc0000005
$ "c:\__w\1\b\build\bin\filecheck.exe" "C:\__w\1\s\CIRCT\circt\test\firtool\split-verilog.mlir" "--check-prefix=VERILOG"
```

It's different tests each time. I've run three builds (2x Release, 1x RelWithDebugInfo) and seen the following tests fail:
```
  CIRCT :: firtool/connect.fir
  CIRCT :: firtool/extract-test-code.fir
  CIRCT :: firtool/firtool.fir
  CIRCT :: firtool/prefixMemory.fir
  CIRCT :: firtool/split-verilog.mlir
  CIRCT :: Dialect/FIRRTL/SFCTests/complex-asserts.fir
  CIRCT :: Dialect/FIRRTL/SFCTests/emit-metadata.fir
```

Unfortunately, debug builds run out of disk space with this particular build agent configuration. (Thank you so much MSVC for being such a disk hog.)

There are two differences which come to mind relative to the CIRCT repo Windows CI builds:
- This one is a unified build.
- This one runs on a 16 core machine

Since different tests are failing for the same commit, this smells like a multithreading problem. I seem to recall this happening in the past on Windows: https://github.com/llvm/circt/issues/376#issuecomment-887919073
